content.cn_text: 第三部分。你将听到两名教育专业学生讨论一项关于教授儿童书写的作业。
content.en_text: Part 3. You will hear two education students discussing an assignment on teaching handwriting

content.cn_text: 首先，你有一些时间查看第21至第24题。
content.en_text: to children. First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听，并回答第21至第24题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 24.

content.cn_text: Man：你关于书写教学的作业进展怎么样？
content.en_text: Man: How are you getting on with the assignment on handwriting?

content.cn_text: Woman：还不错。你知道吗，我以前没意识到孩子学习书写会有这么多好处。
content.en_text: Woman: Not too bad. You know, I haven't realised that children benefit in so many ways from learning to write.

content.cn_text: 这是一项非常重要的技能，但大多数人认为手写不像过去那么重要了，
content.en_text: It's such an important skill and yet most people think handwriting is less important than in the past

content.cn_text: 因为现在人们几乎不怎么手写了。
content.en_text: because people hardly ever write by hand these days.

content.cn_text: Man：是的，而且所有证据都表明，孩子应该先学习手写，再学习打字。
content.en_text: Man: Yes, and all the evidence suggests children should learn to write by hand before they learn to type.

content.cn_text: 很重要的原因之一是它有助于记忆。
content.en_text: Not least because it helps their memory.

content.cn_text: Woman：没错。书写这个身体动作能帮助孩子记住字母。
content.en_text: Woman: That's right. The physical act of writing helps children to remember letters.

content.cn_text: 仔细想想，这一点其实很明显。
content.en_text: That seems pretty obvious when you think about it.

content.cn_text: Man: 不那么明显的是，它如何帮助培养孩子的专注力。
content.en_text: Man: What's less obvious is how it helps develop their concentration.

content.cn_text: 他们必须坐稳，并专注于一件事。
content.en_text: They have to sit still and focus on one thing.

content.cn_text: Woman：是啊，书写的这一方面我以前从没想到过。
content.en_text: Woman: Yeah, that aspect of handwriting had never occurred to me before.

content.cn_text: Man: 嗯，我也是。不过我不太确定它是如何提高孩子想象力的。
content.en_text: Man: Hmm, same here. I'm not sure I understand how it improves children's imagination, though.

content.cn_text: Woman: 不过有一项研究显示，小学年龄段的孩子手写时比使用键盘时能产生更多想法。
content.en_text: Woman: Well, there was that study which showed that primary age children generated more ideas when they were writing by hand than using a keyboard.

content.cn_text: 我倒是能猜到会是这样。
content.en_text: I would have guessed that would be the case.

content.cn_text: Man：嗯，是的。我以前也从没把空间意识和书写联系起来。
content.en_text: Man: Hmm, yeah. I'd never associated spatial awareness with handwriting either.

content.cn_text: 我以为空间意识更多是指知道自己相对于物体或他人的位置。
content.en_text: I thought spatial awareness was more to do with knowing where you are in relation to objects or other people.

content.cn_text: Woman：嗯，我也这么想。
content.en_text: Woman: Hmm, I thought that too.

content.cn_text: 但良好的空间意识对书写很重要，因为你必须正确安排单词之间的间距。
content.en_text: But good spatial awareness is essential for writing because you have to space words correctly.

content.cn_text: 并不只是我一直以为的精细运动技能会通过书写得到提高。
content.en_text: It's not just fine motor skills that improve through writing as I'd always assumed.

content.cn_text: Man：对于有运动协调障碍的孩子来说，书写要困难得多。
content.en_text: Man: Handwriting is so much harder for children with dyspraxia who have problems coordinating movement.

content.cn_text: 好在课堂上有很多方法可以帮助他们。
content.en_text: It's good there are lots of things you can do in the classroom to help them.

content.cn_text: 他们在字母成形方面需要更多支持。
content.en_text: They need so much more support with letter formation.

content.cn_text: 你需要做很多游戏来帮助他们区分字母形状。
content.en_text: You need to play lots of games to help them distinguish letter shapes.

content.cn_text: 这需要很多耐心。
content.en_text: It takes a lot of patience.

content.cn_text: Woman：是的，我喜欢使用那种如果你按得太用力就会亮起来的笔这个想法。
content.en_text: Woman: Yeah, I like the idea of using one of those pens that lights up if you press too hard.

content.cn_text: 这看起来是一个非常简单的解决办法。
content.en_text: That seems like a really simple solution.

content.cn_text: Man：是的，完全同意。
content.en_text: Man: Yes, absolutely.

content.cn_text: 我不确定对于有运动协调障碍的孩子写得很慢这一点，能做多少事情。
content.en_text: I'm not sure there's much you can do about children with dyspraxia writing very slowly.

content.cn_text: 更重要的是关注准确性，随着他们越来越自信，我认为他们最终会写得更快。
content.en_text: It's more important to focus on accuracy and as they get more confident, I think they eventually speed up.

content.cn_text: Woman：一个相当简单的方法是使用方格纸。
content.en_text: Woman: One quite simple thing you can do is to use grid paper.

content.cn_text: 这样他们把每个字母写在一个格子里，这能训练他们正确安排字母间距。
content.en_text: So they write each letter in a box and that trains them to space the letters correctly.

content.cn_text: Man：确实，这对可读性来说比让他们写成一条直线更重要。
content.en_text: Man: Indeed, that's more important for legibility than trying to get them to write in a straight line.

content.cn_text: 在听讨论的剩余部分之前，你有一些时间查看第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Before you hear the rest of the discussion, you have some time to look at questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: 现在请听，并回答第25至第30题。
content.en_text: Now listen and answer questions 25 to 30.

content.cn_text: Man：对一些孩子来说，教他们在笔记本电脑上写作可能比
content.en_text: Man: For some children, it might be better to teach them to write on a laptop rather than

content.cn_text: 手写更好，比如有阅读障碍的孩子。
content.en_text: by hand, like children with dyslexia.

content.cn_text: 他们通常在手写方面非常吃力，有些人干脆放弃。
content.en_text: They often really struggle with handwriting and some just give up.

content.cn_text: Woman：是的，如果他们写错了，也不会那么沮丧。
content.en_text: Woman: Yeah, it's not as frustrating for them if they get things wrong.

content.cn_text: 在键盘上，他们会更愿意尝试。
content.en_text: On a keyboard, they can be more willing to have a go.

content.cn_text: 但我读到过，流利度的发展并不会更快。
content.en_text: But I read that developing fluency isn't any faster.

content.cn_text: Man：没错。
content.en_text: Man: That's right.

content.cn_text: 你读过那篇关于教授印刷体而不是连笔书写的好处的文章吗？
content.en_text: Did you read that article on the benefits of teaching print rather than cursive handwriting,

content.cn_text: 连笔书写就是字母连在一起的那种。
content.en_text: where the letters are joined up?

content.cn_text: Woman：读过。
content.en_text: Woman: Yes.

content.cn_text: 过去，连笔书写真被认为更有风格、更有教养，但现在
content.en_text: Well, in the past, cursive writing was certainly considered more stylish and educated, but not

content.cn_text: 不再是这样了。
content.en_text: anymore.

content.cn_text: 教师的态度已经改变，因为事实证明连笔书写更难学，
content.en_text: Teachers' attitudes have changed because it's been proved that cursive is more difficult

content.cn_text: 尤其是对有学习困难的孩子来说，他们觉得把字母连起来
content.en_text: to learn, especially for children with learning difficulties who find joining up letters

content.cn_text: 非常有挑战性。
content.en_text: really challenging.

content.cn_text: Man：我同意。
content.en_text: Man: I agree.

content.cn_text: 我一直担心我糟糕的书写影响了考试成绩，现在研究
content.en_text: I was always worried that my poor handwriting affected my exam results, and now research

content.cn_text: 表明我的担心是对的。
content.en_text: shows that I was right to worry.

content.cn_text: 我相信很多学生会觉得，因为书写而被评判是不公平的，
content.en_text: I'm sure a lot of students think it's unfair that they're being judged on their handwriting,

content.cn_text: 而不仅仅是因为他们的知识水平。
content.en_text: not just their knowledge.

content.cn_text: Woman：如果考官看不懂答卷，分数肯定会受到影响。
content.en_text: Woman: Marks are definitely affected if examiners can't read the script.

content.cn_text: 这就是为什么教孩子写得清楚一直很重要。
content.en_text: That's why it's always been so important to teach children to write legibly.

content.cn_text: 你认为手写的作用将来会改变吗？
content.en_text: Do you think the role of handwriting will change in the future?

content.cn_text: Man：嗯。
content.en_text: Man: Hmm.

content.cn_text: 我看不出这会有太大变化。
content.en_text: I can't see that changing much.

content.cn_text: 大多数学校仍然不教授触摸打字，这很可惜。
content.en_text: Touch typing still isn't taught in most schools, which is a shame.

content.cn_text: 但也许将来这并不必要，因为人们也能
content.en_text: But maybe that won't be necessary in the future, because people will also be able to write

content.cn_text: 在数字设备上手写。
content.en_text: by hand on digital devices.

content.cn_text: 不管怎样，教师们理解手写的价值。
content.en_text: Anyway, teachers understand the value of handwriting.

content.cn_text: 它是一项基本生活技能。
content.en_text: It's a basic life skill.

content.cn_text: Woman：没错。
content.en_text: Woman: True.

content.cn_text: 不过事实是，人们手写越来越少，越来越依赖数字
content.en_text: However, the fact is that people are writing by hand less and less and relying on digital

content.cn_text: 设备。
content.en_text: devices.

content.cn_text: 这确实会造成一些问题。
content.en_text: That does cause some problems.

content.cn_text: Man：嗯。
content.en_text: Man: Hmm.

content.cn_text: 你是指像记笔记这样的问题吗？
content.en_text: You mean like note-taking.

content.cn_text: 这方面有很多应用程序。
content.en_text: There are lots of apps for that.

content.cn_text: Woman: 显然也有用于阅读历史文献的应用。
content.en_text: Woman: And for reading historical documents, apparently.

content.cn_text: 但我妈妈对我糟糕的拼写以及我标点使用非常不一致这件事感到震惊。
content.en_text: But my mum is shocked by my awful spelling and the fact that my punctuation is really inconsistent.

content.cn_text: 我认为这可以归因于缺乏练习。
content.en_text: I think you can put that down to lack of practice.

content.cn_text: Man：我想是这样。
content.en_text: Man: I expect so.

content.cn_text: 就我个人而言，我怀念手写。
content.en_text: Personally, I miss writing by hand.

content.cn_text: 我现在几乎什么都不手写了。
content.en_text: I hardly ever write anything now.

content.cn_text: 我记得我的祖父母字写得非常漂亮，而且很有个人特色。
content.en_text: I remember my grandparents had such beautiful handwriting, and it was so individual.

content.cn_text: 现在我认识的人中没人能认出我的字迹。
content.en_text: Nobody I know would be able to identify my handwriting now.

content.cn_text: 这挺可惜的。
content.en_text: It's a shame.

content.cn_text: Woman：我知道。
content.en_text: Woman: I know.

content.cn_text: 我也有同感。
content.en_text: I feel the same way.

content.cn_text: 我以前手写日记，现在改成用数字方式写了。
content.en_text: I used to write a diary by hand, and now I do that digitally.

content.cn_text: 那样做似乎更省力。
content.en_text: It just seems less effort to do it that way.

content.cn_text: Man：嗯。
content.en_text: Man: Hmm.

content.cn_text: 所以这不只是一个问题。
content.en_text: So it's not just a problem.

content.cn_text: 第三部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of Part 3.

content.cn_text: 现在你有30秒检查第三部分的答案。
content.en_text: You now have 30 seconds to check your answers to Part 3.

groups.desc:
Choose TWO letters, A–E.
Which TWO benefits for children of learning to write did both students find surprising?
groups.title: Questions 21 and 22.

items.title: Which TWO benefits for children of learning to write did both students find surprising?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题为多选题，问两名学生都觉得惊讶的书写好处之一。原文中提到concentration的作用 less obvious，也提到that aspect had never occurred to me before，所以答案包含 C. improved concentration。 答案解析：第22题为多选题，问两名学生都觉得惊讶的另一个书写好处。男学生说 he'd never associated spatial awareness with handwriting，女学生也说 I thought that too，所以答案包含 E. improved spatial awareness。

items.title: Which TWO benefits for children of learning to write did both students find surprising?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第21题为多选题，问两名学生都觉得惊讶的书写好处之一。原文中提到concentration的作用 less obvious，也提到that aspect had never occurred to me before，所以答案包含 C. improved concentration。 答案解析：第22题为多选题，问两名学生都觉得惊讶的另一个书写好处。男学生说 he'd never associated spatial awareness with handwriting，女学生也说 I thought that too，所以答案包含 E. improved spatial awareness。

groups.desc:
Choose TWO letters, A–E.
For children with dyspraxia, which TWO problems with handwriting do the students think are easiest to correct?
groups.title: Questions 23 and 24.

list.title: B
list.content: not writing in a straight line

list.title: C
list.content: applying too much pressure when writing

items.title: For children with dyspraxia, which TWO problems with handwriting do the students think are easiest to correct?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题为多选题，问对有运动协调障碍的孩子来说，哪两个书写问题最容易纠正。原文说用方格纸可以 train them to space the letters correctly，所以答案包含 A. not spacing letters correctly。 答案解析：第24题为多选题，问另一个较容易纠正的问题。原文说会发光的笔能提示孩子 press too hard，这是 correcting applying too much pressure 的简单方法，所以答案包含 C. applying too much pressure when writing。

items.title: For children with dyspraxia, which TWO problems with handwriting do the students think are easiest to correct?
items.analysis: 答案解析：第23题为多选题，问对有运动协调障碍的孩子来说，哪两个书写问题最容易纠正。原文说用方格纸可以 train them to space the letters correctly，所以答案包含 A. not spacing letters correctly。 答案解析：第24题为多选题，问另一个较容易纠正的问题。原文说会发光的笔能提示孩子 press too hard，这是 correcting applying too much pressure 的简单方法，所以答案包含 C. applying too much pressure when writing。

groups.desc: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.
groups.title: Questions 25–30.

items.title: What does the woman say about using laptops to teach writing to children with dyslexia?

options.title: A
options.content: Children often lack motivation to learn that way.

options.title: C
options.content: Children react more positively if they make a mistake.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第25题问女学生对用笔记本电脑教有阅读障碍儿童写作的看法。原文说 it's not as frustrating if they get things wrong，并且 they can be more willing to have a go，所以答案是 C. Children react more positively if they make a mistake。

items.title: The woman thinks that

options.title: A
options.content: cursive writing disadvantages a certain group of children.

options.title: B
options.content: print writing is associated with lower academic performance.

options.title: C
options.content: most teachers in the UK prefer a traditional approach to handwriting.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第26题问女学生认为连笔书写怎么样。原文说 cursive 对 children with learning difficulties 更难，因为 joining up letters really challenging，所以答案是 A. cursive writing disadvantages a certain group of children。

items.title: According to the students, what impact does poor handwriting have on exam performance?

options.title: A
options.content: There is evidence to suggest grades are affected by poor handwriting.

options.title: B
options.content: Neat handwriting is less important now than it used to be.

options.title: C
options.content: Candidates write more slowly and produce shorter answers.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第27题问书写差对考试表现有什么影响。原文说 research shows that I was right to worry，并且 Marks are definitely affected if examiners can't read the script，所以答案是 A. There is evidence to suggest grades are affected by poor handwriting。

items.title: What prediction does the man make about the future of handwriting?

options.title: A
options.content: Touch typing will be taught before writing by hand.

options.title: B
options.content: Children will continue to learn to write by hand.

options.title: C
options.content: People will do all handwriting on digital devices.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第28题问男学生对手写未来的预测。原文说 I can't see that changing much，并且 handwriting is a basic life skill，说明孩子仍会学习手写，所以答案是 B. Children will continue to learn to write by hand。

items.title: The woman is concerned that relying on digital devices has made it difficult for her to
items.analysis: 答案解析：第29题问女学生担心依赖数字设备让她在哪方面变困难。原文说 her awful spelling and inconsistent punctuation，所以答案是 B. spell and punctuate。

items.title: How do the students feel about their own handwriting?

options.title: A
options.content: Though legible, it could be more stylish.

options.title: B
options.content: Children will continue to learn to write by hand.

options.title: C
options.content: Embarrassed they have let the habit lapse.

items.analysis: 答案解析：第30题问两名学生对自己书写的感受。男学生说 I miss writing by hand, I hardly ever write anything now，女学生说 I feel the same way，并提到过去手写日记现在改成数字方式，说明他们尴尬或遗憾自己放弃了这个习惯，所以答案是 C. Embarrassed they have let the habit lapse。
